//: AGENT-PAY-1 — a RECORDED Stripe TEST-MODE charge RESPONSE, authored tightly against Stripe's documented Charge object shape (docs.stripe.com/api/charges/object). This is the byte the gateway must EXTRACT the charge amount FROM: the cost it reserves/settles against the cross-rail cap is `amount_captured` (cents), NOT a number pre-supplied in the transcript. `livemode:false` marks this as a test-mode charge (no real money). The gateway is bound-not-build (PRD §11): it never charges Stripe — it reads this response shape (what Stripe's agent-toolkit MCP server returns) and meters the captured amount. rail=stripe.
//extract: the amount the cross-rail counter meters is `charge.amount_captured` = 300 cents ($3.00); the receipt must name the charge id `ch_3MmlLrLkdIwHu7ix0snN0B15` so a stranger can re-derive the metered cost from the recorded rail response (NOT from a transcript-supplied cost_cents).
